# Argus

> Argus is search infrastructure for AI agents. 14 providers with topology-aware routing, Reciprocal Rank Fusion ranking, WolframAlpha computed answers, 12-step content extraction with completeness assessment, universal provenance tracking (egress/machine/source), retrieval workflows, corpus storage, and budget enforcement. Works immediately with DuckDuckGo + Yahoo — no API keys needed. Add API keys for 7,000+ free monthly queries. Connect via HTTP, CLI, MCP server, or Python import.

## What It Does

> You pass Argus a search query. It fans out to multiple providers (prioritizing free ones), collects results, ranks them via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60), deduplicates by URL, and returns a clean list. If a provider is down or over budget, it skips it automatically. Your code never touches a provider API directly.
> Output is designed for LLM consumption — title, snippet, URL, domain, provider, and relevance score for each result.

## Installation

> pip install argus-search works immediately with DuckDuckGo + Yahoo on any machine with Python 3.11+
> pip install "argus-search[mcp]" adds MCP server support
> pip install "argus-search[mcp,crawl4ai]" adds local JS rendering (needs playwright + crawl4ai)
> Docker: docker compose up -d for SearXNG + Argus (SearXNG disabled by default — enable in .env when you have a container running)

## Providers

> Tier 0 (free): SearXNG (self-hosted, disabled by default — enable if Docker is running), DuckDuckGo (no key), Yahoo (scraped), GitHub (free), WolframAlpha (2k/mo computed answers)
> Tier 1 (monthly recurring): Brave (2k/mo), Tavily (1k/mo), Exa (1k/mo), Linkup (1k/mo)
> Tier 3 (one-time credits): Serper (2.5k), Parallel AI (4k), You.com ($20), Valyu ($10), SearchAPI
> Routing: always queries free providers first, then monthly, then one-time. Exhausted providers skipped. Topology-aware routing optimizes for residential egress (homelab) automatically.

## Content Extraction

> 12-step fallback chain with quality gates and completeness assessment:
> auth (cookie-based paywall bypass) → trafilatura (local, fast) → Crawl4AI (local JS rendering) → Obscura (stealth headless browser) → Playwright (local headless Chrome) → residential (remote IP) → Jina Reader (external API) → Valyu Contents ($0.001/URL) → Firecrawl (1 credit/page) → You.com Contents ($1/1k pages) → Wayback Machine → archive.is
> Completeness assessment runs after every step: detects feed truncation (trailing ellipsis, "Read more" markers, mid-sentence endings, abrupt paragraphs, word caps). Confidence >= 0.85 triggers chain continuation for free extractors.
> First 6 steps need local install. Last 6 are external APIs (work anywhere).
> POST /api/assess-content to check completeness of text you already have without fetching.
> SSRF protection blocks private IPs. Domain rate limiting at 10 req/min/domain. Cookie auth for paywalls.

## Retrieval Workflows

> argus workflows recover-article — find and extract a lost/dead article
> argus workflows capture-site — snapshot a site into local corpus
> argus workflows build-research-pack — multi-query research with local artifact persistence
> Corpus storage via platformdirs (or ARGUS_DATA_ROOT override).
> MCP progress notifications: capture_site, build_research_pack, and recover_dead_article report progress back to Claude/Codex sessions via ctx.report_progress().

## Multi-Turn Sessions

> Pass session_id to any search call. Broker stores queries and extracted URLs in SQLite. Follow-up searches are context-enriched from prior conversation. Persists across restarts.

## Integration

### HTTP API

> argus serve on port 8000. POST /api/search {"query": "...", "mode": "discovery", "max_results": 10, "session_id": "..."}
> POST /api/extract {"url": "..."}. POST /api/recover-url {"url": "...", "title": "..."}
> POST /api/assess-content {"text": "...", "url": "..."}. POST /api/expand {"query": "..."}
> GET /api/health/detail, GET /api/budgets. OpenAPI at /docs.

### CLI

> argus search -q "query" --mode discovery --session my-session --json
> argus extract -u "https://example.com" -d nytimes.com
> argus recover-url -u "https://dead.link" -t "Title"
> argus health, argus budgets, argus doctor, argus set-balance -s jina -b 9833638, argus test-provider -p brave
> argus mcp init, argus mcp check, argus mcp serve
> argus workflows recover-article / capture-site / build-research-pack

### MCP

> argus mcp serve (stdio) or argus mcp serve --transport streamable-http --port 8001 (Antigravity-compatible)
> Tools: search_web, extract_content, recover_url, expand_links, search_health, search_budgets, test_provider, valyu_answer, cookie_health
> Resources: argus://providers/status, argus://providers/budgets, argus://policies/current, argus://corpus/paths
> Config: {"mcpServers": {"argus": {"command": "argus", "args": ["mcp", "serve"]}}}
> MCP Registry: io.github.Khamel83/argus

### Python

> from argus.broker.router import create_broker; from argus.models import SearchQuery, SearchMode
> from argus.extraction import extract_url
> broker = create_broker()
> response = await broker.search(SearchQuery(query="...", mode=SearchMode.DISCOVERY, max_results=10))
> for r in response.results: print(f"{r.title}: {r.url} (score: {r.score:.3f})")
> content = await extract_url("https://example.com")

## Search Modes

> discovery: related pages and canonical sources. Free providers always lead.
> recovery: find moved or dead URLs via archive sources.
> grounding: fact-checking with live sources + WolframAlpha computed answers.
> research: broad exploratory retrieval with WolframAlpha for factual grounding.

## Configuration

> All config via ARGUS_ prefix env vars. Key variables: ARGUS_SEARXNG_BASE_URL (also ARGUS_SEARXNG_ENABLED=true to enable), provider API keys (ARGUS_BRAVE_API_KEY etc.), ARGUS_CACHE_TTL_HOURS (168), per-provider budgets (ARGUS_BRAVE_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD etc.), ARGUS_CRAWL4AI_ENABLED, ARGUS_EXTRACTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS.
> See .env.example for full list. Missing keys degrade gracefully — providers are skipped, not errors.

## Architecture

> Caller (CLI/HTTP/MCP/Python) → SearchBroker (topology-aware) → routing policy (tier-sorted, egress-policy) → provider executor (budget check → health check → search → provenance injection) → cache → dedupe → RRF ranking → response
> 12-step content extraction with quality gates, completeness assessment, and adaptive domain memory (SQLite).

## Documentation

> README.md — product page with quickstart, providers, integration, configuration
> CONTRIBUTING.md — contribution guide and adding providers
> CLAUDE.md — developer guide and architecture overview
> AGENTS.md — AI contributor onboarding
